menu.title: SUDOKU PRO
menu.subtitle: Vue 3 / Nuxt · Step-by-step hint system
resume.title: Saved game found
resume.message: You have a saved {difficulty} game at {time}. Continue it or start a new one?
difficulty.title: Choose a Challenge
difficulty.subtitle: Pick your difficulty level:
difficulty.beginnerDesc: Most cells given — learn the grid with confidence.
difficulty.easyDesc: Simple eliminations only — great for building intuition.
difficulty.mediumDesc: Requires pointing pairs and basic techniques.
difficulty.hardDesc: Naked pairs, hidden singles and more.
difficulty.masterDesc: Near-minimal clues — demands expert chains and deep logic.
game.whyConflict: {num} can't go here — it clashes with the {num} in row {r}, column {c}. A digit may appear only once per row, column and box.
game.whyWrong: No visible clash, but {num} isn't the digit for this cell — the puzzle would become unsolvable. Tip: use notes (N) to narrow the candidates.
hint.panelDesc: Click the Hint button during the game to activate deep logical analysis and step-by-step deconstruction of complex solving techniques.
hint.move.none: No further logical move found from here.
hint.move.naked-single.name: Naked Single
hint.move.naked-single.desc: This cell has only one candidate left — {num}. Every other digit already appears in its row, column, or box.
hint.move.hidden-single.name: Hidden Single
hint.move.hidden-single.desc: {num} fits in only one cell of this unit, so it must go there.
hint.move.pointing.name: Pointing Pair
hint.move.pointing.desc: Within one box, {digits} is confined to a single line — so it can be removed from the rest of that line ({count} cell(s)).
hint.move.box-line.name: Box-Line Reduction
hint.move.box-line.desc: Within one line, {digits} is confined to a single box — so it can be removed from the rest of that box ({count} cell(s)).
hint.move.naked-pair.name: Naked Pair
hint.move.naked-pair.desc: Two cells share only {digits}, locking those digits. Remove them from {count} other cell(s) in the unit.
hint.move.hidden-pair.name: Hidden Pair
hint.move.hidden-pair.desc: {digits} appear in only two cells of a unit, so their other candidates can be removed.
hint.move.naked-triple.name: Naked Triple
hint.move.naked-triple.desc: Three cells together hold only {digits}. Remove those digits from {count} other cell(s) in the unit.
hint.move.hidden-triple.name: Hidden Triple
hint.move.hidden-triple.desc: {digits} are locked to three cells of a unit, so their other candidates can be removed.
hint.move.naked-quad.name: Naked Quad
hint.move.naked-quad.desc: Four cells together hold only {digits}. Remove those digits from {count} other cell(s) in the unit.
hint.move.hidden-quad.name: Hidden Quad
hint.move.hidden-quad.desc: {digits} are locked to four cells of a unit, so their other candidates can be removed.
hint.move.x-wing.name: X-Wing
hint.move.x-wing.desc: {num} forms an X-Wing across two lines, so it can be removed from the crossing lines ({count} cell(s)).
hint.move.swordfish.name: Swordfish
hint.move.swordfish.desc: {num} forms a Swordfish across three lines, so it can be removed from the crossing lines ({count} cell(s)).
hint.move.jellyfish.name: Jellyfish
hint.move.jellyfish.desc: {num} forms a Jellyfish across four lines, so it can be removed from the crossing lines ({count} cell(s)).
hint.move.xy-wing.name: XY-Wing
hint.move.xy-wing.desc: An XY-Wing forces {num} out of {count} cell(s) seen by both pincers.
hint.move.xyz-wing.name: XYZ-Wing
hint.move.xyz-wing.desc: An XYZ-Wing forces {num} out of {count} cell(s) seen by the pivot and both pincers.
modal.winHintMsg: You solved the puzzle with the analyzer in {time}.
modal.winInstantMsg: You solved the puzzle with the analyzer!
customImport.title: Custom Puzzle
customImport.description: Paste 81 digits (0 = empty cell). Spaces and newlines are ignored.
customImport.errorBoxDuplicate: Box at row {row} col {col} has duplicate {value}.
customImport.errorNoSolution: This puzzle has no solution — check the digits and try again.
customImport.errorMultipleSolutions: This puzzle has more than one solution, so mistakes could not be checked fairly. Add more givens.
stats.title: Your Statistics
stats.subtitle: Lifetime progress, saved on this device
stats.noTechniquesYet: No techniques used yet — request a hint in a game to start tracking.
stats.noStatsYet: No games won yet. Finish a puzzle to start building your stats!
academy.title: Sudoku Academy
academy.subtitle: The {n} techniques the analyzer uses — each with a real worked example
academy.tapHint: Tap a card to see it worked out
academy.footer: These are exactly the techniques the in-game analyzer uses. When you request a hint, it applies them from Basic to Expert and shows the same step-by-step deduction.
academy.tier.ExpertDesc: Wing patterns built from bivalue chains.
academy.caption.nakedSingle: {cell} (green) has only one candidate left — {num}. Every other digit is already in its row, column, or box, so {num} is placed here.
academy.caption.hiddenSingle: Across this unit, {num} can only go in {cell} (green) — the other empty cells (amber marks) never list it. So {num} must go there.
academy.caption.pointing: Inside one box, every candidate for {digits} lies on a single line ({cells}, indigo). So {digits} is removed from the rest of that line — {nElim} cell(s) in rose ({elimList}).
academy.caption.boxLine: In one line, {digits} is confined to a single box ({cells}, indigo). So {digits} is removed from the rest of that box — {nElim} cell(s) in rose ({elimList}).
academy.caption.nakedSubset: These {k} cells (indigo) hold only the digits {digits} between them, locking them. They're removed from {nElim} other cell(s) in the unit — rose ({elimList}).
academy.caption.hiddenSubset: The digits {digits} appear only in these {k} cells (indigo) of the unit, so they must live here — every other candidate in those cells is removed.
academy.caption.fish: {num} forms this fish across {lines} lines (indigo corners), locking it into those lines. So {num} is removed from the crossing lines — {nElim} cell(s) in rose ({elimList}).
academy.caption.xyWing: A pivot and two pincers (indigo) chain so that one pincer must be {num}. Any cell seeing both pincers loses {num} — {nElim} cell(s) in rose ({elimList}).
academy.caption.xyzWing: A 3-candidate pivot and two pincers (indigo) share {num}. One of the three must be {num}, so a cell seeing all three loses it — {nElim} cell(s) in rose ({elimList}).
academy.tech.naked-single.lookFor: A cell where only one digit is a valid candidate — every other digit already appears in its row, column, or box.
academy.tech.naked-single.howItHelps: Place that single remaining candidate. No deduction needed; the answer is forced by elimination.
academy.tech.hidden-single.lookFor: Within a row, column, or box, a digit that can only fit in one cell — even if that cell has other candidates too.
academy.tech.hidden-single.howItHelps: The digit has nowhere else to go in that unit, so it must go there. Place it.
academy.tech.pointing.lookFor: Inside a box, all candidates for a digit lie on a single row or column.
academy.tech.pointing.howItHelps: The digit must land on that line within the box, so remove it from the rest of that row or column outside the box.
academy.tech.box-line.lookFor: All candidates for a digit within a row or column are confined to a single box.
academy.tech.box-line.howItHelps: The digit must live in that line, so remove it from every other cell of that box.
academy.tech.naked-pair.lookFor: Two cells in the same unit that each hold exactly the same two candidates and no others.
academy.tech.naked-pair.howItHelps: Those two digits fill those two cells in some order. Remove both from every other cell in the unit.
academy.tech.hidden-pair.lookFor: Two digits that appear as candidates in exactly two cells of a unit, and nowhere else in it.
academy.tech.hidden-pair.howItHelps: Those two cells must hold those two digits, so all their other candidates can be removed.
academy.tech.naked-triple.lookFor: Three cells in a unit whose combined candidates are exactly three digits (each cell holds two or three of them).
academy.tech.naked-triple.howItHelps: Those three digits are locked into those three cells. Eliminate them from every other cell in the unit.
academy.tech.hidden-triple.lookFor: Three digits that appear only across three cells of a unit, and nowhere else in it.
academy.tech.hidden-triple.howItHelps: Those three cells must contain those three digits, so strip all their other candidates.
academy.tech.naked-quad.lookFor: Four cells in a unit whose combined candidates are exactly four digits.
academy.tech.naked-quad.howItHelps: Those four digits are confined to those four cells. Eliminate them from all other cells in the unit.
academy.tech.hidden-quad.lookFor: Four digits that appear only within four cells of a unit.
academy.tech.hidden-quad.howItHelps: Those four cells own those four digits, so all their other candidates can be removed.
academy.tech.x-wing.lookFor: A digit with exactly two candidate cells in each of two rows (or columns), all sharing the same two columns (or rows).
academy.tech.x-wing.howItHelps: The digit is forced into those two lines, so remove it from those columns (or rows) everywhere else.
academy.tech.swordfish.lookFor: Like an X-Wing but across three rows (or columns), with all candidates spanning exactly three columns (or rows).
academy.tech.swordfish.howItHelps: The digit is locked into those three lines, so remove it from the crossing lines everywhere else.
academy.tech.jellyfish.lookFor: A four-line X-Wing: a digit across four rows (or columns) confined to the same four columns (or rows).
academy.tech.jellyfish.howItHelps: The digit is locked into those four lines, so remove it from the crossing lines everywhere else.
academy.tech.xy-wing.lookFor: Three bivalue cells: a pivot (X, Y) sharing one candidate with each of two pincers (X, Z) and (Y, Z).
academy.tech.xy-wing.howItHelps: Whatever fills the pivot, one pincer becomes Z. Any cell seeing both pincers loses Z.
academy.tech.xyz-wing.lookFor: Like an XY-Wing, but the pivot holds three candidates (X, Y, Z) and the two pincers are (X, Z) and (Y, Z).
academy.tech.xyz-wing.howItHelps: One of the three cells must be Z, so any cell seeing all three loses Z.
